mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces borrowing costs for corporations, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly positive during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, weak labor market conditions signal severe economic distress, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series rise, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly higher following positive INDPRO momentum, or the signal reverses direction during recovery phases, yielding a negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stagnant federal funds rate indicate unanchored inflation expectations and delayed monetary tightening, prompting equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction between high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative returns, or the signal becomes positive when inflation rises without rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 persons signals strengthening labor demand and economic resilience, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of months where payroll growth exceeds 100k, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 changes remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates a synchronized supply and labor contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.
